Ntc Of Motion By Deft Marcus Daniel Merchasin In Support Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Document Production Demands By Pltff Mario Galande And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2475 Against Pltff Mario Galande And His Attorney
Off Calendar Nov-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Steven F. Helfand, Esq. of Helfand Law Offices, Tel. 415.397.0007, for defendant and moving party;
Rey Hassan, Esq. for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MARCUS MERCHASIN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Document Production Demands By Plaintiff Mario Galande And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2475 Against Plaintiff Mario Galande And His Attorney Rey Hassa Jointly And Severally. The motion is granted. Plaintiff Mario Galande shall serve further verified responses to all requests for production in which Plaintiff responded, "The documents requested are in possession of the Defendant and will be included in this production." Plaintiff shall amend the responses to state that all responsive, non-privileged documents in the custody, control or possession of the Plaintiff have been produced. If there are additional responsive, non-privileged documents in Plaintiff's custody or control that have not yet been produced, those documents shall be produced when the further responses are served. Plaintiff shall provide code-compliant, responsive, verified further responses to requests for production Nos. 16, 27, 28, and 37. Plaintiff shall provide a privilege log for all documents withheld from production related to request for production No. 52. Plaintiff's further responses and any additional documents required by this order shall be served on Defendant no later than November 27, 2014. Plaintiff opposed Defendant's motion without substantial justification and did not meet his obligations to meet and confer on these issues. Accordingly, Plaintiff Mario Galande and Plaintiff's counsel, Rey Hassan, are sanctioned, jointly and severally, in the amount of $750, payable to Defendant no later than December 17, 2014. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maximillian Contreras, CSR# 13876, Tel. 415.987.5746.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Rey Hassan, Esq. of the Law Office of Rey Hassan, Tel. 415.775.9700, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs WILLIAM HEDDEN, MARCUS MERCHASIN and CONSOLIDATED ADJUSTING, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendant's reply relies on case law holding that litigation ending with a negotiated settlement is not a favorable termination because it does not reflect the merits of the underlying action but instead is the result of a compromise between the parties. Ferreira v. Gray, Cary, Ware & Freidenrich (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 409, 413; see also Dalany v. American Pac. Holding Corp., (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 822, 828-29. This reliance is misplaced. Both of the cases cited by Defendant terminated as a result of negotiated settlements. Here, in contrast, the parties litigated the action to a final judgment on the merits. The $16,323 payment to Defendant does not change the character of the termination of this action because the malicious prosecution alleged by Plaintiff relates to the $1 million default judgment awarded to and pursued for over ten years by Defendant subsequent to Defendant's receipt of the amount that the parties agreed that Defendant was owed. Thus, Plaintiff did not challenge nor seek return of that amount in its action. Moreover, the judgment in the underlying case finds that the $16,323 was a full settlement for the 10% agreed on by the parties as well as the $7000 advanced to Plaintiff. Likewise this judgment should not effect the character of Plaintiff as the "prevailing party" in the underlying case because this amount was, if ever, negotiated and settled prior to the alleged malicious prosecution by Defendant for the $1 million judgment. The judgment in the underlying case specifically holds that the Plaintiff (now Defendant) "takes nothing." Consolidated Adjusting v. Miller-Galande Antiques (2010) Case No. 302473 Judgment at 1; see also Complaint 53, 54, 60, 61 (pleading elements). All requests for judicial notice are granted. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
